summary: Recent updates to the image testing capabilities in Firefox bring important enhancements, particularly around the handling of **JPEG XL (JXL)**, a modern image format that offers better compression and quality. Notable changes include support for Adobe-style extensions in JPEG XL, adjustments to test conditions for different platforms, and improvements that ensure testing is more robust on Android devices. In addition, there are refinements to the way images are compared during tests, which helps catch inconsistencies and enhances the overall reliability of image processing in Firefox. These improvements matter because as web content becomes increasingly image-centric, ensuring that browsers accurately render and process images is crucial for a seamless user experience.
